title: Separate Shed and Tub Change into independent buttons on animal detail cards

requirements.text: On the animal detail card UI (the AnimalCard shown per animal), replace the single combined "Shed & Tub Change" action with two independent buttons displayed side-by-side in the same button grid section: one for logging a Shed event and one for logging a Tub Change event. Each button must be clickable independently so the user can record either event or both when they occur at different times.

quotes: Can you separate the tub change button and shed button so I can hit either one or both of it’s not at the same time?
The animal detail page only I don’t need this in bulk actions.
2 independent buttons each logging their own event
Have them sit side by side in the same section

acceptanceCriteria: In AnimalCard, the combined "Shed & Tub Change" button is removed and replaced by two buttons labeled clearly (e.g., "Log Shed" and "Log Tub Change") displayed side-by-side in the same actions area.
Clicking "Log Shed" creates only a shed record for that animal; clicking "Log Tub Change" creates only a tub change record for that animal.
Both buttons show appropriate loading/disabled state while their respective mutation is pending and show success/error toast messages that match the action taken.
No changes are made to bulk actions dialogs or their UI.

requirements.text: Add or wire up dedicated mutations for logging a Shed event and logging a Tub Change event from the frontend. If the backend does not already expose single-action endpoints, add backend methods to record shed-only and tub-change-only events for a given animal ID using the current time, and expose them in the frontend actor + React Query hooks.

quotes: 2 independent buttons each logging their own event

acceptanceCriteria: Frontend has two separate mutation hooks usable by AnimalCard: one that logs shed-only and one that logs tub-change-only.
Triggering either mutation invalidates/refetches the relevant per-animal history queries and the derived "animalsWithEvents"/days-since metrics so UI badges and history update after logging.
Backend methods (if added) enforce the same access control rules as existing animal event logging and do not affect other event types.
Existing combined logging functionality (if currently used elsewhere) continues to work or is cleanly replaced without breaking other screens.

requirements.text: Update the Shed/Tub Change history UI to correctly reflect entries that may be shed-only, tub-change-only, or both. Specifically, adjust the list item label and delete/edit dialog copy so it does not always say "Shed & Tub Change" when only one of the two event types exists for that timestamp grouping.

quotes: separate the tub change button and shed button so I can hit either one or both

acceptanceCriteria: In the ShedTubChangeList, each rendered history row indicates what is present for that row (Shed only, Tub Change only, or Shed & Tub Change).
Delete confirmation text and success/error toasts reflect what is being deleted (shed, tub change, or both) based on the combined entry contents.
Edit dialog title/description similarly reflects whether the user is editing a shed date, a tub change date, or both, while still allowing editing of whichever records exist in that combined entry.

constraints: Use English for all user-facing text.
Do not modify bulk action workflows; changes apply only to the animal detail card/page UI.
Do not edit files under frontend/src/components/ui or any other immutable paths listed in SYSTEM_CONTEXT.
Backend must remain single-actor (all Motoko logic in backend/main.mo); add migration.mo only if state schema changes require it.
nonGoals: Fixing or changing the Safari TLS/HTTPS connection issue shown in the uploaded screenshot.
Changing how shed/tub entries are stored historically beyond what is required to support separate logging.
Adding new bulk logging actions for shed or tub change.
